screenshots.description: Instruction hierarchy diagram showing how configuration files, skill files, and auto memory load at different times with different context costs, arranged as a layered pyramid
scenario: Generate pyramid diagram with three tiers
Top: Config files (always loaded, full cost)
Middle: Skill files (on demand, near-zero cost until used)
Bottom: Auto memory (session start, first 200 lines)
Show context cost indicators on each tier
screenshots.caption: The three-tier instruction hierarchy showing when each mechanism loads and its context cost
screenshots.alt_text: Pyramid diagram showing three agent instruction mechanisms: configuration files loaded always at full context cost, skill files loaded on demand at near-zero cost, and auto memory loaded at session start

capture_order.name: localhost-dependent web captures
capture_order.notes: Start Open Design, OpenPencil, Remotion Studio, Hyperframes instances first

capture_order.name: external web captures by domain
capture_order.notes: Group by domain to minimize navigation. Already logged in to all services.

capture_order.name: desktop captures
capture_order.notes: Group by app (VS Code). screencapture fallback since agent-desktop unavailable.

capture_order.name: terminal captures
capture_order.notes: Render as styled terminal HTML using visual-explainer, then capture screenshot.

capture_order.name: visual-explainer composites
capture_order.notes: Generate styled HTML renders, then capture screenshots.

capture_order.name: AI-generated diagrams
capture_order.notes: No dependencies. Generate last. Use create-image for each diagram prompt.

risks.description: Open Design, OpenPencil, Remotion Studio, and Hyperframes require local instances running for web captures. These may not be installed or may have version mismatches.
risks.mitigation: Install and verify each tool before starting batch 1. Fallback: use text descriptions and tables already in chapter HTML.

risks.description: agent-desktop is not installed (confirmed in environment.json). Desktop captures rely on screencapture which produces whole-screen shots requiring manual cropping.
risks.mitigation: Use screencapture with specific window targeting (-w flag). Crop with sips. Allow extra time for manual cleanup.

risks.description: Paper Desktop MCP server must be running at http://127.0.0.1:29979/mcp for ch05-fig-02 and ch10-fig-04 captures. Server only runs when Paper Desktop app is open with a file loaded.
risks.mitigation: Launch Paper Desktop and load a design file before starting terminal captures. Verify MCP endpoint responds.

risks.description: AI-generated diagrams (batch 6) may not match the exact visual style needed for the book. Multiple iterations may be required.
risks.mitigation: Use detailed prompts with style references. Allow 2-3 iterations per diagram. Fallback to text descriptions.

risks.description: Some visual-explainer composite renders (batch 5) depend on having actual tool output to compare. Mock data may look unconvincing.
risks.mitigation: Prioritize captures from actual tool sessions. Use real output where possible, mock only for side-by-side comparisons.

risks.description: VS Code dark mode is enabled (environment.json). All desktop captures must be switched to light theme first.
risks.mitigation: Switch VS Code to light theme before desktop capture batch. Verify with a test capture before proceeding.
